items.title: Tesla’s ‘Full Self-Driving’ fraud lawsuit gets first hearing in China — 10 owners seek $583K
items.topic: Tesla’s ‘Full Self-Driving’ fraud lawsuit gets first hearing in China — 10 owners seek $583K
items.summary: Tesla’s ‘Full Self-Driving’ fraud lawsuit gets first hearing in China — 10 owners seek $583K. The lawsuit, filed by Tesla owners, alleges that the company's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology is not as advanced as advertised, raising concerns about consumer trust and safety.
items.abstract: Tesla’s ‘Full Self-Driving’ fraud lawsuit gets first hearing in China — 10 owners seek $583K. The lawsuit, filed by Tesla owners, alleges that the company's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology is not as advanced as advertised, raising concerns about consumer trust and safety.

items.title: Tesla’s Full Self-Driving software is creeping into Europe
items.topic: Tesla’s Full Self-Driving software is creeping into Europe
items.summary: Tesla’s Full Self-Driving software is creeping into Europe. Tesla is expanding its Full Self-Driving (FSD) software to European markets, marking a significant step in the global rollout of its advanced driver assistance system (ADAS). This move could influence regulatory standards and consumer adoption in the region.
items.abstract: Tesla’s Full Self-Driving software is creeping into Europe. Tesla is expanding its Full Self-Driving (FSD) software to European markets, marking a significant step in the global rollout of its advanced driver assistance system (ADAS). This move could influence regulatory standards and consumer adoption in the region.

items.title: Tesla FSD v14 is so good it’s making me dangerously complacent
items.topic: Tesla FSD v14 is so good it’s making me dangerously complacent
items.summary: Tesla FSD v14 is so good it’s making me dangerously complacent. A Tesla owner reports that the latest version of Full Self-Driving (FSD) v14 is performing so well that it is causing the driver to become overly relaxed, highlighting the potential risks of over-reliance on autonomous driving systems.
items.abstract: Tesla FSD v14 is so good it’s making me dangerously complacent. A Tesla owner reports that the latest version of Full Self-Driving (FSD) v14 is performing so well that it is causing the driver to become overly relaxed, highlighting the potential risks of over-reliance on autonomous driving systems.

items.title: NVIDIA Launches Cosmos 3, the Open Frontier Foundation Model for Physical AI
items.topic: NVIDIA Launches Cosmos 3, the Open Frontier Foundation Model for Physical AI
items.summary: NVIDIA Launches Cosmos 3, the Open Frontier Foundation Model for Physical AI. NVIDIA has released Cosmos 3, an open-source foundation model for physical AI, aimed at advancing research and development in areas such as robotics and autonomous vehicles. This model is designed to improve perception, decision-making, and control in physical environments.
items.abstract: NVIDIA Launches Cosmos 3, the Open Frontier Foundation Model for Physical AI. NVIDIA has released Cosmos 3, an open-source foundation model for physical AI, aimed at advancing research and development in areas such as robotics and autonomous vehicles. This model is designed to improve perception, decision-making, and control in physical environments.

items.title: Illinois Lawmakers Just Passed America’s Strongest AI Safety Bill
items.topic: Illinois Lawmakers Just Passed America’s Strongest AI Safety Bill
items.summary: Illinois Lawmakers Just Passed America’s Strongest AI Safety Bill. Illinois has enacted a comprehensive AI safety bill, setting a new standard for AI regulation in the United States. The bill includes provisions for transparency, accountability, and ethical use of AI, which could have significant implications for the development and deployment of autonomous vehicles.
items.abstract: Illinois Lawmakers Just Passed America’s Strongest AI Safety Bill. Illinois has enacted a comprehensive AI safety bill, setting a new standard for AI regulation in the United States. The bill includes provisions for transparency, accountability, and ethical use of AI, which could have significant implications for the development and deployment of autonomous vehicles.

items.title: Tesla patents camera wiper for self-driving — resulting in more doubts for FSD owners
items.topic: Tesla patents camera wiper for self-driving — resulting in more doubts for FSD owners
items.summary: Tesla patents camera wiper for self-driving — resulting in more doubts for FSD owners. Tesla has patented a camera wiper system for its self-driving vehicles, but the move has raised additional doubts among Full Self-Driving (FSD) owners about the reliability and necessity of the technology.
items.abstract: Tesla patents camera wiper for self-driving — resulting in more doubts for FSD owners. Tesla has patented a camera wiper system for its self-driving vehicles, but the move has raised additional doubts among Full Self-Driving (FSD) owners about the reliability and necessity of the technology.

items.title: Why Tesla’s AI trainers don’t trust its self-driving tech – or its safety stats
items.topic: Why Tesla’s AI trainers don’t trust its self-driving tech – or its safety stats
items.summary: Why Tesla’s AI trainers don’t trust its self-driving tech – or its safety stats. Reuters reports that Tesla's own AI trainers have expressed skepticism about the company's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology and the safety statistics provided by Tesla, raising concerns about the reliability and transparency of the system.
items.abstract: Why Tesla’s AI trainers don’t trust its self-driving tech – or its safety stats. Reuters reports that Tesla's own AI trainers have expressed skepticism about the company's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology and the safety statistics provided by Tesla, raising concerns about the reliability and transparency of the system.

items.title: Tesla’s own AI trainers don’t trust ‘Full Self-Driving’ or its safety stats, Reuters finds
items.topic: Tesla’s own AI trainers don’t trust ‘Full Self-Driving’ or its safety stats, Reuters finds
items.summary: Tesla’s own AI trainers don’t trust ‘Full Self-Driving’ or its safety stats, Reuters finds. Tesla's internal AI trainers have reportedly expressed significant doubts about the company's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology and the safety statistics it provides, highlighting internal concerns about the system's reliability.

items.title: Run Step 3.7 Flash on NVIDIA GPUs with Enterprise-Ready Multimodal AI | NVIDIA Technical Blog
items.topic: Run Step 3.7 Flash on NVIDIA GPUs with Enterprise-Ready Multimodal AI | NVIDIA Technical Blog
items.summary: Run Step 3.7 Flash on NVIDIA GPUs with Enterprise-Ready Multimodal AI | NVIDIA Technical Blog. NVIDIA has detailed a new method for running Step 3.7 Flash on its GPUs, enabling more efficient and enterprise-ready multimodal AI applications. This advancement could enhance the performance and capabilities of AI systems in autonomous vehicles.
items.abstract: Run Step 3.7 Flash on NVIDIA GPUs with Enterprise-Ready Multimodal AI | NVIDIA Technical Blog. NVIDIA has detailed a new method for running Step 3.7 Flash on its GPUs, enabling more efficient and enterprise-ready multimodal AI applications. This advancement could enhance the performance and capabilities of AI systems in autonomous vehicles.

items.title: Google’s new anything-to-anything AI model is wild
items.topic: Google’s new anything-to-anything AI model is wild
items.summary: Google’s new anything-to-anything AI model is wild. Google has unveiled a new AI model capable of converting between different types of data, such as text, images, and audio. This versatile model could have significant applications in various fields, including autonomous driving, by improving perception and decision-making systems.
